products.title: Three products. One mission.
products.subtitle: Software, hardware, and everything in between. Each tool solves a real problem we've lived ourselves.

bfr.name: BusFileReader
bfr.desc: The modern DBC, ARXML & LDF editor your toolchain is missing. Read, explore, analyze — fast, beautiful, free.

obd.name: OBD Remote Bridge
obd.desc: Run ODIS, ISTA, or Techstream on a vehicle 500km away. Encrypted OBD2 tunnel with physical J2534 passthrough.

autobridge.name: AutoBridge
autobridge.desc: Modular multi-bus automotive interface. CAN, CAN-FD, LIN, K-Line, FlexRay, Automotive Ethernet. One platform, every bus.

bfr_features.title: Your DBC files deserve better.
bfr_features.subtitle: 500 frames, zero lag. Multi-format. Dark mode. Everything you need to explore vehicle networks, nothing you don't.
bfr_features.bit_title: See exactly what's on the wire.
bfr_features.bit_desc: Bit-level signal visualization. Every signal mapped, colored, packed. Motorola or Intel byte order — displayed correctly, always.
bfr_features.fmt_desc: One interface, one data model — DBC today, ARXML when you need it. Every automotive bus format, no context-switching.

pricing.title: Every price is the real price.
pricing.subtitle: No "contact sales". No surprise quotes. No enterprise tax. What you see is what everyone pays.
pricing.free_note: Full DBC experience. CAN & CAN FD, from hobbyist to production.
pricing.pro_note: For AUTOSAR engineers and professional workflows. ARXML, FIBEX, LDF — the formats that run at Tier 1.
pricing.pro_f6: Full editing, diff & merge, validation reports

community.title: Built with you, not for you.
community.subtitle: Open roadmap. Direct feedback. No support tickets at 200€/hour — just a Discord where we actually talk.
community.msg1_content: Just pushed v0.2.0-beta — ARXML parsing is now live. Still rough around the edges on some ETAS exports, but the core chain works. Grab it and let us know what breaks.
community.msg2_content: Tested on a 35MB ARXML from our ISOLAR project. Parses in 3.2s, all frames visible. Signal encoding looks correct. This is already better than what I had before.
community.msg3_content: Any plans for LDF support? We're a small EV shop and our LIN configs are a pain to manage without proper tooling.
community.msg4_content: LDF is on the roadmap right after ARXML stabilizes. Architecture is ready for it — the parser plugs into the same unified model. Probably 2-3 months out.

news.eyebrow: Reading material for real CAN engineers
news.title: What's on the bus

article1.title: CAN Bus, DBC & ARXML: The Formats Behind Automotive Network Data
article1.excerpt: Behind every CAN frame lies a precise structure — defined by a DBC or ARXML file your tool needs to parse without choking. DBC covers messages, signals, units and enumerations. ARXML handles the full AUTOSAR communication stack. BusFileReader supports both — including the undocumented OEM variants that nobody writes specs for.

article2.title: Decoding a CAN Log File: IDs, DLC and Intel vs Motorola Endianness
article2.excerpt: Opening a raw .log or .blf shouldn't require a PhD in binary voodoo. BusFileReader handles Intel and Motorola endianness, factor/offset decoding, and multi-gigabyte logs without breaking a sweat — including the quirky exports from certain OEM tools. Spoiler: if the decoding looks wrong, it's probably not your file.
